data.title: Iron Ore Falls; Fundamentals Appear Strong — Market Talk
data.description: Iron ore falls in early Asian trade. The ferrous metal's fundamentals appear to have strong support, say Nanhua Futures analysts in a note. On the valuation side, market sentiment has recovered after recent data showed strong steel consumption in China, they write. Investors are also awaiting China's National People's Congress meeting in March for any additional property-supportive measures. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.4% lower at 794.5 yuan a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Sats' Attributable Profit Surges 124% in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Sats' attributable profit to owners surged 124% in the fiscal quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, to SG$70.4 million from SG$31.5 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Revenue was up 13% year over year to SG$1.52 billion from SG$1.35 billion, as gateway services and food solutions segments reported a 10% and 21% improvement from the year-ago period.

Shares of the company were down nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Indian benchmarks set to open lower on global demand, tariff woes
data.description:
Indian benchmark stock indexes are set to open lower on Monday, tracking weakness in their global peers, after the U.S. equities tumbled in the previous session on concerns over softening consumer demand and tariff threats.

The GIFT Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 22,680.0, as of 07:29 a.m. IST, indicating that blue-chip Nifty 50 will open below Friday's close of 22,795.90.

U.S. consumer sentiment hit a 15-month low in February and inflation expectations rocketed due to President Donald Trump's tariff plans, as per latest data.

Most Asian markets fell, tracking the losses in the U.S. on Friday amid growth concerns.

The fear of stagflation in the U.S. is the biggest negative catalyst for markets, Mehta Equities said.

Stagflation, which refers to slowing growth and rising prices, in the world's largest economy does not bode well for export-driven sectors such as the information technology segment in India.

It also makes India and other emerging markets unattractive for foreign investors as they may prefer safer assets such as the dollar and treasuries.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) have sold Indian shares worth $11.77 billion so far in 2025, including 34.49 billion rupees ($398.2 million) on Friday, as per provisional data.

India's benchmark indexes are down as much as 13% from their record highs hit in late September last year due to concerns over slowing earnings growth and Trump's tariff threats.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** National Stock Exchange of India said Jio Financial Services and Zomato will replace Bharat Petroleum Corp and Britannia Industries in the Nifty 50.

** Private equity firm KKR will acquire up to 54% stake in Healthcare Global Enterprises at 445 rupees per share, and will conduct an open offer.

** Swiggy will invest $115.47 million in its supply chain unit Scootsy, amid an ongoing expansion of its fast-growing quick-commerce arm Instamart.

data.title: Jane Fonda honored with 2025 SAG Life Achievement Award
data.description:
Jane Fonda, the two-time Oscar winning actor and activist with a career spanning six decades and genres across film and television, was awarded the SAG life achievement award on Sunday at the 31st Screen Actors Guild (SAG) Awards. The ceremony was streamed live on Netflix.

"Your enthusiasm makes this seem less like the twilight of my life and more like a 'Go girl kick ass.' Because I'm not done," Fonda said.

"For a woman like me, who grew up in the 40s and 50s, when women weren't supposed to have opinions and not get angry, acting gave me a chance to play angry women with opinions, which, as you know, is a bit of a stretch for me," she added.

Fonda also applauded SAG for being a union that creates empathy and gives actors the chance to understand humanity and "touch souls."

The actor also took the time to address the United States political landscape with President Trump's administration.

"We must help the vulnerable. We must find ways to project an inspiring vision of the future," Fonda added, noting films that she's been in that have pushed political boundaries.

The 87-year-old is best known for the 1971 film “Klute,” 1980’s film “9 to 5” and the 2004 movie “Monster-in-Law."

Her more recent appearances have included in the Netflix comedy series "Grace and Frankie" in which she and Lily Tomlin play older women finding new life after divorce.

The New York native is also seven-time Golden Globe Winner who is known for her work in the Broadway show, "33 Variations" in 2009, which won her a Tony for best actress.

Fonda is also known as a political activist, earning the nickname Hanoi Jane for her campaigns against the Vietnam War in the 1970s. She later campaigned against the Iraq War in 2003, and in recent years has held "Fire Drill Fridays" protests highlighting the dangers of global warming.

"It's hard to separate Jane the activist from Jane the actress. She is ferocious in her advocacy and in her art," Julia Louis-Dreyfus said before handing Fonda her award.

The daughter of late actor Henry Fonda and sister of late actor Peter Fonda has become a household name within Hollywood.

Fonda is also a producer, author and fitness guru who has almost claimed EGOT status, meaning she is close to winning an Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony in her career.

She was nominated for a Grammy in 1983 for Best Spoken Word Or Non-Musical Recording for her "Workout Book for Pregnancy, Birth, and Recovery," but did not win to obtain EGOT status.

data.title: South Korea's Exports Jump in First 20 Days of February
data.description:
South Korea's exports jumped 16% to $35.3 billion in the first 20 days of February from a year earlier, data from the Korea Customs Service indicated Friday.

The increase in outbound shipments was due to more working days of 15.5 during the period as compared to 13 a year ago.

Imports rose 7.7% to $34.5 billion, leading to an $800 million trade surplus.

data.title: NZ's Chorus slips on swinging to half-yearly loss
data.description:
** Shares of Chorus down 3.8% to NZ$8.59

** Stock falls as much as 7.1% earlier in the day to NZ$8.3, posting its biggest intraday pct loss since early July 2020

** Telecom service provider posts net loss after tax of NZ$5 mln ($2.88 mln) for HY25 vs a profit of NZ$5 mln in the same period last year

** Expects FY25 EBITDA to track to lower half of its estimate range of NZ$700 mln-NZ$720 mln

** More than 485,000 shares change hands, 4.4 times the 30-day avg

** Stock down 2.5% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.7367 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Market Chatter: Samsung to Showcase Medical Imaging Tech at ECR 2025
data.description:
Samsung Electronics' medical device unit and subsidiary Samsung Medison will showcase their latest imaging technology at the 2025 European Congress of Radiology (ECR) in Vienna from Wednesday, Feb. 26, to Sunday, March 2, Pulse News reported Friday.

Their booth will feature pediatric ultrasound devices, the new GF85 fixed X-ray system, and the OmniTom Elite PCD CT scanner, the report said.

Samsung also plans to highlight its S-Hub power management system.

Shares of Samsung Electronics fell nearly 2% in recent trade on Monday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Baazar Style Retail Opens New Store in Assam, India
data.description:
Baazar Style Retail (NSE:STYLEBAAZ, ) has opened a new store at Paltan Bazar in Assam, India, on Saturday, according to a same-day disclosure to the Indian bourses.

With this, the company's total number of stores has increased to 203.

data.title: US Presses China on Economic Concerns in Introductory Call
data.description:
In his first call with China's Vice Premier He Lifeng, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent voiced concerns over economic imbalances, unfair policies and counternarcotics efforts to reaffirm the US' commitment to safeguarding its economy and national security.

Both officials agreed to maintain communication, according to a Feb. 21 news release from the US Treasury Department.

data.title: German stock futures, euro jump on Merz win
data.description:
Key points:
  • DAX up, Euro hits 1-mth high as Merz wins German vote
  • Wall St futures bounce ahead of Nvidia earnings
  • Asia stocks dip, trade thin with Tokyo on holiday

European shares and the euro climbed on Monday as Germany's election produced no nasty surprises, while Wall Street futures firmed on hopes results from AI diva Nvidia this week would justify the tech sector's sky-high valuations.

DAX futures jumped 1.1%, while the single currency rose 0.5% to $1.0516 and looked set to test its January top at $0.10535. EUROSTOXX 50 futures added 0.4% and FTSE futures 0.1%.

German's new conservative leader Friedrich Merz still has to form a coalition government and it is not yet clear whether that will include one or two partners, with the latter likely to take more time and horse trading.

The uncertainty comes as European Union leaders are set to hold an extraordinary summit on March 6 to discuss additional support for Ukraine and how to pay for European defence needs.

Liquidity was thinned by a holiday in Tokyo markets and MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) dipped 0.2%. Nikkei futures (NKc1) traded at 38,310, under the cash close of 38,776.

Chinese blue chips eased 0.1%, but Hong Kong shares firmed 0.2% to extend their recent tech-driven bull run.

S&P 500 futures added 0.4% and Nasdaq futures 0.5%. The Nasdaq had fallen 2.5% last week, its worst week in three months, with losses led by the Magnificent Seven.

That pullback raised the stakes for Nvidia's results on Wednesday where investors are looking for fourth-quarter sales around $38.5 billion and first-quarter guidance around $42.5 billion.

As usual, options point to a share price move of around 8% in either direction should the results surprise.

Wall Street had taken a hit on Friday when a survey on services showed a shock slide in activity amid concerns about tariffs and cost pressures. There were even reports the White House was pressuring Mexico to put its own tariffs on Chinese imports as part of a deal.

INFLATION SCARE

The Federal Reserve's favoured measure of core inflation is due on Friday and expected to show a slowdown to 2.6% from 2.8%, but could be overshadowed by tariff worries.

A survey of U.S. consumers out on Friday showed inflation expectations for 5 to 10 years ahead climbed to 3.5%, the highest since 1995.

"Longer-run inflation expectations are at risk of becoming de-anchored," warned analysts at ANZ in a note. "That was the clear message from the subset of soft U.S. economic surveys released on Friday, and for the Fed, the data signal that enhanced caution is required."

At least nine Fed officials are speaking this week, some of them more than once, and are likely to reiterate the cautious message on further rate cuts.

Markets are not priced for an easing until July, with just two cuts implied all year. (0#USDIRPR)

Treasuries rallied in the wake of the soft services data, though inflation and supply uncertainties remain a hurdle and 10-year futures (YTc1) were down 5 ticks on Monday.

The drop in Treasury yields, particularly in real terms, has weighed on the dollar against the yen as Japanese yields rise on speculation of another rate hike from the Bank of Japan.

The dollar was pinned at 149.36 yen , having shed 2% last week to threaten chart support at 148.65. The dollar index dipped 0.3% to 106.210, with losses on the euro, sterling and Swiss franc.

In commodity markets, gold remained well supported at $2,935 an ounce , having climbed for eight weeks in a row.

Oil has been heading in the other direction in part on speculation an eventual peace deal on Ukraine could see sanctions eased on Russia, boosting its oil exports.

Brent was 11 cents higher at $74.54 a barrel, while U.S. crude added 1 cent to $70.42 per barrel having hit a two-month low earlier.

data.title: Palm Oil Falls Amid Weaker Soybean Oil, Palm Olein — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading, tracking weakness in soybean oil last Friday on the Chicago Board of Trade, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Palm olein's softness on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is also adding pressure to palm oil, it reckons. Major consumer India may raise vegetable oil import taxes to support struggling local oilseed farmers, which could boost local prices but curb imports of palm oil, soybean oil and sunflower oil, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,610 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,705 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 51 ringgit to 4,613 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: CALB Group Investing 2 Billion Euros in Portugal Gigafactory
data.description:
CALB Group will invest 2 billion euros in a Portuguese gigafactory, producing 15 gigawatt hours of lithium batteries yearly, powering about 187,000 electric vehicles, by 2028, Reuters reported Sunday.

The Sines plant will create 1,800 jobs and contribute over 4% to Portugal's gross domestic product at full capacity, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: British Business - February 24
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- Axa IM Alts, the investment arm of the French insurer Axa , has submitted a planning application to build another skyscraper in the City of London.

- Healthcare provider Bupa is to offer what is claimed to be the first genetic test capable of accurately predicting the risk of common diseases in an attempt to improve prevention in the UK.

The Guardian

- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced 200 million pounds ($253.54 million) in funding to boost investment at Grangemouth oil refinery, which is closing down with the loss of more than 400 jobs.

- UK's Home Office has been accused of collecting data on "hundreds of thousands of unsuspecting British citizens" while conducting financial checks on migrants.

The Telegraph

- BBC bosses will be grilled by senior MPs over a controversial documentary accused of offering a propaganda platform for Hamas.

Sky News

- British tech company Quantexa is in talks to secure an undisclosed amount of new funding from the Ontario Teachers Pension Plan which could value the company at billions of dollars.

($1 = 0.7888 pounds)

data.title: Riverstone's Profit, Revenue Rise in Q4 2024
data.description:
Riverstone's attributable profit to equity holders rose to 70.0 million Malaysia ringgit during the fourth quarter of 2024, from 67.5 million ringgit a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share increased to 0.0473 ringgit compared with 0.0456 ringgit in the comparable period.

Revenue increased to 278.0 million ringgit from 229.8 million ringgit, mainly due to improved average selling price and volume.

The company recommended a special interim dividend of 0.04 ringgit per share and a final dividend of 0.08 ringgit per share for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024.

data.title: Gold prices retreat as traders lock in profits; US data in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Bullion hit a record high last week
  • U.S. inflation data due on Friday
  • Gold still eyeing all-time highs this week - analyst

Gold prices retreated on Monday as traders locked in profits after recent record highs, with attention shifting to a key U.S. inflation report set for release later this week.

Spot gold was down 0.4% at $2,925.63 an ounce, as of 0204 GMT. Bullion scaled an all-time high of $2,954.69 on Thursday.

U.S. gold futures dipped 0.5% to $2,939.30.

"Gold has been unable to capitalise on the broader risk-averse theme of the market, with profit-taking moves offsetting any rise in safe-haven demand," said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

"With trade uncertainties not looking like they are disappearing anytime soon, gold could still be eyeing off reaching new all-time highs again this week."

U.S. President Donald Trump said last week that he would announce fresh tariffs over the next month or sooner, adding lumber and forest products to previously announced plans to impose duties on imported cars, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals.

This follows the already-imposed additional 10% levy on Chinese imports and a 25% tariff on steel and aluminium.

Traders are closely monitoring the U.S. Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory for clues, as Trump's policies are seen as inflationary.

If inflationary pressures lead the Fed to keep interest rates high, gold's appeal as a non-yielding asset could diminish.

Markets will shift focus towards the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index, the Fed's preferred inflation measure, due on Friday for more confirmation of the central bank's rate path.

On the geopolitical front, Trump reversed course on Friday and said Russia did in fact invade Ukraine, and that Kyiv would soon sign a minerals agreement with the United States as part of efforts to end the Ukraine war.

Spot silver was down 0.4% at $32.42 an ounce, platinum dropped 0.2% to $967.71, and palladium shed 0.3% to $965.97.

data.title: ASX 200 stocks hitting fresh 52-week highs and lows – Week 9
data.description:
Welcome back to the 52-week Series– A recap of S&P/ASX 200 stocks that have tagged a fresh yearly highs and lows in the past week. By tracking these 52-week highs and lows, we can spot emerging trends across different stocks and sectors.

When we see clusters of stocks hitting new milestones within a sector, it often signals significant underlying factors – whether it's shifting commodity prices, evolving demand trends (like the current AI boom), or companies exceeding or falling short of earnings expectations.

52-Week Highs and Lows by Sector

  • Financials: 7 Highs, 1 Low

  • Industrials: 6 Highs, 3 Lows

  • Discretionary: 5 Highs, 0 Lows

  • Materials: 4 Highs, 5 Lows

  • Technology: 3 Highs, 1 Lows

  • Staples: 3 Highs, 0 Lows

  • Health Care: 2 Highs, 1 Low

  • Real Estate: 2 Highs, 1 Low

  • Telecommunication: 2 Highs, 0 Lows

  • Energy: 0 Highs, 2 Lows

  • Utilities: 0 Highs, 1 Low

What Does the Data Tell Us?

  • Fading Strength: While 34 stocks hit fresh 52-week highs, momentum appears to be slowing, particularly in the banking sector. Financials tumbled sharply last week, weighed down by disappointing trading updates from key players like Westpac, Bendigo Bank, and NAB. The broader weakness suggests that upward momentum is stalling, likely due to poor forward guidance, stretched valuations, and macroeconomic headwinds.

  • Rising Lows: The number of stocks hitting 52-week lows has remained relatively stable over the past month, ranging from 5 to 8. However, this week saw a sharp increase to 14, largely driven by weaker-than-expected earnings results.

  • Industrial Strength: Many industrial stocks reaching 52-week highs have delivered stronger-than-expected earnings, despite operating in different sub-sectors. Brambles, for example, slightly beat 1H25 earnings expectations due to cost efficiencies and also upgraded its free cash flow guidance.

  • Materials Struggle: Lithium and coal stocks continue to slump, pressured by weak commodity prices and poor operational performance. MinRes shares plunged 20.7% last week after the company unexpectedly raised capex guidance, citing severe weather disruptions that led to a downgrade in iron ore shipment forecasts.

data.title: South Korean Won Rises on Weak US Dollar
data.description:
The South Korean won rose to around 1,428 per dollar, remaining at its highest level in a month, primarily attributed to a weakening greenback.

The US dollar weakened amid concerns about a slowing US economy and persistent inflation, which could prompt the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

In South Korea, investors eagerly awaited the Bank of Korea's upcoming monetary policy decision later this week.

The central bank is widely expected to implement a 25-basis-point rate cut, following its surprising decision to maintain the key interest rate at 3% during its January 2025 meeting.

Meanwhile, investors continued to closely monitor US President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariff threats, following South Korean officials' request for an exemption from US steel and aluminum tariffs during their visit to Washington last week.

data.title: KKR to Acquire Controlling Stake in Indian Hospital Operator for $400 Million
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

KKR has agreed to buy a controlling stake in Indian hospital operator HealthCare Global Enterprises for $400 million, adding to its recent investments in the country's healthcare industry.

The U.S. investment company said Sunday that it would acquire a stake of up to 54% in HealthCare Global from CVC Capital Partners and assume sole control of its operations.

HealthCare Global founder BS Ajaikumar will become nonexecutive chairman, focusing on clinical, academic and research and development work, the companies said.

Founded in 1989, Bengaluru-based HealthCare Global is one of India's largest cancer-care providers, operating 25 medical-care centers.

Akshay Tanna, partner and head of India private equity at KKR, said its investment in HealthCare Global would support the development of medical infrastructure and the delivery of oncology services in the country. Healthcare continues to be a thematic focus for the firm in India, he said.

KKR recently invested in Baby Memorial Hospital in the southern Indian state of Kerala and Bengaluru-based medical devices company Healthium Medtech.

KKR said it would buy shares of HealthCare Global from CVC at 445 rupees, equivalent to $5.14, apiece. The U.S. investment company will also start an open offer to purchase additional shares in the hospital operator from public shareholders.

Upon completion of the offer, KKR is expected to hold an equity stake in HealthCare Global of between 54% and 77%, it said.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: COMMENT-Bank of Thailand to hold rates amid external risks
data.description:
The Bank of Thailand (BOT) is likely to maintain its policy rate at 2.25% on Wednesday, despite below-forecast fourth-quarter growth, as external uncertainties and U.S. trade tariff risks limit the room for swift rate cuts .

Thai Q4 GDP rose 3.2% from a year earlier, taking 2024 GDP growth to 2.5% from 2.0% in 2023 . While below the 3.9% forecast, Q4 growth was still the strongest annual rate in nine quarters, and the government remains optimistic on 3% growth this year .

Rising tourism, increased consumer confidence and resilient public spending are expected to support the economy. Tourism, a key source of earnings for Thailand, is likely to improve further after arrivals from China returned to the top spot in 2024.

Thai consumer confidence hit an eight-month high in January on stimulus measures . The industrial sentiment index also rose to a 10-month high last month, bolstered by government stimulus, exports and tourism .

Headline inflation rose 1.32% annually in January from 1.23% in December, but remains within the BOT's 1% to 3% target range . The BOT governor said the current policy interest rate remains appropriate .

The central bank held rates steady in December after a surprise cut in October . With one of the lowest policy rates in the region, the BOT has limited room for monetary easing to cushion the economy in a downturn.

Moreover, there is no urgency for a rate cut as the U.S. Federal Reserve is seen on hold in the near term after January's hot inflation print .

USD/THB has come off its January highs and concerns on U.S. economic growth and lower oil prices on geopolitical peace hopes should support the baht. USD/THB may consolidate between 33.0-34.0 into the second half of 2025.

For more click on

data.title: Djerriwarrh Investments Posts Higher Fiscal H1 Profits Despite Lower Revenue
data.description:
Djerriwarrh Investments reported Monday that its profits per diluted share in the fiscal first half rose to AU$0.0814, from AU$0.049 per diluted share a year earlier.

Revenue for the six months of Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$18.8 million, down from AU$19.5 million in the same period last year.

On Feb.5, the company declared an interim dividend of AU$0.0725 per share payable on Monday, Feb. 24 to shareholders of record as of Jan. 31.

data.title: Zeria Pharmaceutical Profit Slips in Fiscal Nine Months to December 2024
data.description:
Zeria Pharmaceutical reported a 2.3% decline in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 8.23 billion yen for the fiscal first nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 8.42 billion yen a year ago mainly due to absence of large extraordinary income.

The pharmaceutical group's basic earnings per share (EPS) fell to 186.63 yen from 191.09 yen a year ago, according to a delayed filing.

Net sales increased 11.8% to 64.8 billion yen from 57.9 billion yen a year earlier.

It forecasted net profit to grow 16.4% to 9 billion yen or 204.18 yen per basic share and net sales to gain 14.2% to 86.5 billion yen for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.

It declared an interim dividend of 23 yen, with a full-year dividend forecast of 46 yen per share.

data.title: Japan Excellent Net Income Falls 11% Between H1, H2 2024
data.description:
Japan Excellent reported a nearly 11% decline in net income between the first half and the second half of 2024 to 3.74 billion yen from 4.20 billion yen.

The company's earnings per unit (EPU) fell to 2,770 yen in H2 from 2,862 yen in H1, according to a stock exchange filing last Friday.

Total revenue increased more than 11% on a seasonally adjusted basis to 12.8 billion yen from 11.46 billion yen in the previous period.

For the fiscal period ending June 30, the company expects net income to grow to 4.32 billion yen or 3,000 yen per unit, while net sales are expected to decline 10.92% to 11.36 billion yen.

It declared a dividend of 2,770 yen per unit, payable.

The company expects to pay 3,000 yen per unit for the period ending June 30, and 2,800 yen per unit for the period ending Dec 31, 2025.

data.title: Soccer-Matildas coach happy with improved effort despite loss to US
data.description:
Matildas coach Tom Sermanni was pleased with an improved effort but said there was still much work to be done after the Australia women lost 2-1 to the United States at the SheBelieves Cup in Arizona on Sunday.

Sermanni described the performance in the 4-0 loss to Japan in the opening match of the tournament as "un-Australian" and a similar humbling looked on the cards when Lynn Biyendolo gave an experimental U.S. side the lead inside a minute.

The dominance of the number one ranked Americans at Glendale's State Farm Stadium was again rewarded when Michelle Cooper doubled their lead with a cool finish in the 68th minute.

The Australians kept to their task, however, and substitute striker Michelle Heyman pulled a goal back with a header from Hayley Raso's cross in the 80th minute.

"I think effort and attitude were so much better in this game, especially after the start we had," Scot Sermanni told reporters.

"Given the performance of the last game and the result, and then losing the goal after probably less than a minute, I think we could easily have just sort of caved in today.

"From a perspective of the team digging in and sticking in there, I was really pleased with part of our performance and that effort."

Australia face Colombia in their final match of the invitational tournament on Wednesday.

"I think we do need to still keep the ball better and our movement needs to be a bit slicker, rhythm needs to be better," Sermanni said.

"I felt that we were basically bullied by the Japanese. So get back to getting closer to teams when we're trying to press, and to bring that physical element of the game back.

"We want to be dynamic in possession but, at the same time, we want to have an edge about our game that the Australian teams in any sport have."

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Extend Gains to Begin the Week
data.description:
Hong Kong equities rose 76 points or 0.3% to 23,551 in early Monday trading, marking a second session of gains and reaching their highest level since early February 2022.

The property sector led the advance, climbing over 2.5%, driven by gains in Sun Hung Kai Properties (4.3%), China Overseas Land (3.1%), and Henderson Land Development (2.4%).

Financials also traded higher after Beijing urged increased services spending across multiple industries last week.

However, gains were limited by renewed US-China trade tensions after reports that US President Donald Trump instructed the Committee on Foreign Investment to curb Chinese investments in sectors like energy and other critical industries.

On the corporate front, Alibaba Group and Chinese state-owned funds expressed interest in DeepSeek’s funding round for the first time.

Other notable movers included Horizon Robotics (11.5%), Sands China (4.2%), and Semiconductor Manufacturing Intl. (2.9%).

data.title: Alibaba to invest more than $52 billion in AI over next 3 years
data.description:
Alibaba said on Monday it plans to invest at least 380 billion yuan ($52.44 billion) in its cloud computing and artificial intelligence infrastructure over the next three years.

The Chinese e-commerce giant had said it had plans to invest in the sector while announcing its results on Friday, but did not provide an exact figure at the time.

The company had reported revenue of 280.15 billion yuan for the three months ended December 31, marginally ahead of analysts' estimates.

Alibaba said the total investment amount exceeds the company's spending in AI and cloud computing over the past decade.

The company has kicked off 2025 as a winner in China's AI race, drawing in investors with strategic business deals. Its stock has risen more than 68% this year, as of last close.

Other Chinese firms have also been investing into the sector, with ByteDance, the Chinese owner of TikTok, earmarking over 150 billion yuan in capital expenditure for this year, much of which will be centred on AI according to sources familiar with the matter, Reuters reported in late January.

($1 = 7.2470 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: FTSE Straits Times Index Erases Loss; Last 0.4% Higher at 3944.67
Japan Excellent Plans Up to 4 Billion Yen Buyback
data.description:
Japan Excellent plans to repurchase and subsequently cancel investment units to boost investor value by increasing the net asset value per unit and dividends per unit.

The Reit plans to acquire up to 35,000 units, or 2.65% of the total outstanding units, with a maximum acquisition price of 4 billion yen, according to its filing last Friday.

The acquisition will be executed through a market purchase on the Tokyo Stock Exchange from Feb. 25 to May 30.

All acquired units will be cancelled on June 27, and the Reit will provide updates once the exact number of units for cancellation is determined.

data.title: Petronas Chemicals' Target Price Cut to MYR5.80 From MYR6.20 by Citi >5183.KU
New Zealand Credit Card Spending Rises 1.3% in January
data.description:
Credit card spending in New Zealand rose 1.3% month on month to NZ$4.62 billion in January following a 1.1% increase in December 2024, data from the Reserve Bank showed Monday.

Domestic billings on New Zealand-issued cards rose 1.4% to NZ$3.78 billion following a 0.6% increase. Billings on overseas-issued cards rose to NZ$843 million from NZ$834 million.

Compared with the year-earlier period, credit card spending rose 1.3% in January compared with a 1.3% decline in December 2024.

data.title: Market Chatter: SAIC Motor Partners with Huawei to Develop Smart EVs
data.description:
SAIC Motor is teaming up with tech company Huawei to develop advanced smart electric vehicles, Reuters reported Feb. 22.

The collaboration will focus on manufacturing, supply chain management, and sales services, according to the report.

Huawei has been expanding its automotive footprint with partnerships including Changan , Dongfeng , and BAIC .

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)
